rationale: V5 is committed after V4 full-corpus evaluation and manual review of the single V4 SBOM_INCONSISTENT finding. The finding was metadata.component.version 'v0.3.1' versus wheel metadata version '0.3.1'. PEP 440 says a preceding single 'v' character is ignored and the same version with and without 'v' is equivalent. V5 therefore adds a narrow version-normalization subset before comparing wheel-scoped SBOM component versions. V4 results remain process evidence and are not overwritten.
frozen: This file is committed before the V5 full-corpus re-evaluation. Categories, file-level classifications, wheel-level verdict rules, sampling policy, and falsification criteria may not change after V5 full-corpus results are observed. Amendments require a new versioned file with rationale; V1 through V5 remain in the repository as process evidence.

checker_scope: JSON parseability for JSON SBOM files
file-level component scope classification from CycloneDX metadata.component.purl when present
fallback wheel-scope inference from CycloneDX metadata.component.name when purl is absent
wheel-scoped metadata.component.name compared with wheel metadata name
wheel-scoped metadata.component.version compared with wheel metadata version after the V5 narrow version-normalization subset
not_claimed: does not validate full CycloneDX or SPDX schemas
does not compare every SBOM component to every wheel file
does not treat non-PyPI component SBOMs as contradictions against wheel metadata
does not trust, merge, or replace SPIRA evidence with embedded SBOM contents
does not perform vulnerability, license, malware, provenance, or legal analysis

file_level_classification.PYPI_WHEEL_SCOPED: metadata.component.purl starts with pkg:pypi/ and the purl package name is compared with the wheel package name
file_level_classification.PYPI_WHEEL_SCOPED_INFERRED: metadata.component.purl is absent and metadata.component.name matches the wheel package name after PEP 503 normalization
file_level_classification.COMPONENT_SCOPE_NOT_WHEEL: metadata.component.purl exists but is not pkg:pypi/, for example pkg:cargo/ or pkg:deb/
file_level_classification.UNKNOWN_COMPONENT_SCOPE: metadata.component.purl is absent and metadata.component.name does not match the wheel package name, or the component is missing
file_level_classification.UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT: SBOM format or standard is outside the V1 checker subset
file_level_classification.SBOM_PARSE_INVALID: embedded JSON SBOM cannot be parsed as UTF-8 JSON or the JSON root is not an object

wheel_level_categories.SBOM_CONSISTENT: At least one wheel-scoped SBOM exists and every wheel-scoped SBOM checked by SPIRA V1 is consistent with wheel metadata.
wheel_level_categories.SBOM_INCONSISTENT: At least one wheel-scoped SBOM has a positive name or version contradiction against wheel metadata.
wheel_level_categories.NO_WHEEL_SCOPED_SBOM: Embedded SBOM files exist, but none are classified as wheel-scoped under V5 rules.
wheel_level_categories.SBOM_INVALID: An embedded JSON SBOM file could not be decoded as UTF-8 JSON or its JSON root was not an object. This is parseability invalidity only, not full schema invalidity.
wheel_level_categories.NOT_EVALUATED: Embedded SBOM evidence is present but outside SPIRA's V1 checker scope and no wheel-scoped consistency decision was made.
wheel_level_categories.TOOL_ERROR: SPIRA crashed or produced unreadable output. This is a SPIRA bug or harness bug, never a finding about the package.

category_rules: Every selected wheel receives exactly one wheel-level survey category.
File-level COMPONENT_SCOPE_NOT_WHEEL is never a wheel-level contradiction.
A wheel is SBOM_INCONSISTENT only on a positive contradiction in a wheel-scoped SBOM.
Absence of wheel-scoped SBOM data is NO_WHEEL_SCOPED_SBOM, not SBOM_INCONSISTENT.
If a wheel contains multiple embedded SBOM files, non-wheel-scoped SBOMs are recorded as file-level evidence while wheel-level consistency is decided only from wheel-scoped SBOMs.
If a wheel has one consistent wheel-scoped SBOM and one non-wheel-scoped Cargo SBOM, the wheel-level PEP 770 V1 category is SBOM_CONSISTENT.
A leading single 'v' or 'V' version prefix and surrounding whitespace are ignored for version comparison under the V5 PEP 440 normalization subset.
SPIRA raw statuses are preserved in raw results; survey categories are a reporting layer over those raw statuses.

external_validation.historical_observation: The March 2026 sbomify top-15k analysis reported invalid embedded SBOMs in that corpus, attributed to a cargo-cyclonedx issue.
external_validation.v4_note: Cargo-scoped SBOMs are not contradictions merely because their component name or version differs from the PyPI wheel. V4 only treats wheel-scoped SBOM contradictions as SBOM_INCONSISTENT.

sampling.snapshot_manifest: Use research/pep770_survey/results/snapshot_v3_2026-07-09_manifest.json as the V5 full-evaluation corpus source.
sampling.full_evaluation: Target is all SELECTED wheels from the snapshot manifest. If runtime, storage, or rate-limit constraints require sampling before full evaluation, a methodology.v6.json must be committed before evaluating sampled results.

reporting_rules: Named packages with wheel-level SBOM_INCONSISTENT or SBOM_INVALID findings are not published before contacting maintainers.
NO_WHEEL_SCOPED_SBOM aggregate counts are public because they are scope/coverage findings, not package accusations.
The final report must include not_claimed language for security, malware, vulnerability, legal, energy, and full schema validation claims.
The report must include a methodology note that the pilot found false positives caused by treating Cargo-scoped SBOMs as if they described the PyPI wheel.
All scripts, methodology files, raw aggregate results JSON, snapshot manifest, and generated aggregate tables are committed under research/pep770_survey/.
